description: The figure presents a bar chart comparing machine-learning-predicted and experimentally measured Vickers microhardness values for three arc-cladded Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel alloys labeled Sample 1, Sample 2, and Sample 3. The blue bars denote SVR-model predicted values, whereas the pink bars indicate experimental measurements, with error bars representing measurement variability and black percentage labels quantifying the prediction error for each alloy. Specifically, Sample 1 shows a predicted hardness of 521 HV against an experimental value of 515 HV (1.17 % error), Sample 2 registers 490 HV predicted versus 500 HV experimental (2.00 % error), and Sample 3 registers 415 HV predicted versus 424 HV experimental (2.09 % error). The close agreement across all three distinct compositions, highlighted by a minimum deviation of only 1.17 %, supports the core conclusion that the integrated machine learning framework—combining support vector regression, CGANs synthetic data augmentation, and firefly optimization—achieves extreme high accuracy in predicting and experimentally validating the microhardness of Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si clad steels.
